channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Appeals court denies bid to block public release of special counsel's report on Trump Jan. 6 probe
item.description: A federal appeals court has denied a bid to block the public release of special counsel Jack Smith’s report on President-elect Donald Trump’s efforts to overturn his 2020 election loss.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic Sen. John Fetterman to meet with Trump at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pennsylvania Sen. John Fetterman will become the chamber's first Democrat to meet with President-elect Donald Trump since the election and plans to travel to Trump's private Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michelle Obama doesn't attend state funeral for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama did not attend the state funeral Thursday for former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: America's newest governor takes page from Trump with DOGE-like commission
item.description: Republican Kelly Ayotte of New Hampshire, America's newest governor, appears to take a page from President-elect Trump by proposing a state level DOGE-like government efficiency commission
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Emhoff appear to ignore Trump, Obama as outgoing veep grimaces at presidential banter
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and President-elect Donald Trump apparently did not acknowledge each other at the Washington National Cathedral during Jimmy Carter's state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House passes ICC sanctions bill; GOP Senate leader promises to bring it to floor
item.description: The House on Thursday passed legislation to sanction officials with the International Criminal Court (ICC), pushing back against the court's decision to issue arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his former defense minister for their actions during Israel’s war against H
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden eulogizes ‘great American’ Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Thursday eulogized former President Carter, hailing the 39th president as a man of character who left an enduring legacy both during his time in the White House and after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Obama exchange pleasantries at Carter funeral after long-standing rivalry
item.description: President-elect Trump and former President Obama had an extended friendly exchange as they sat next to each other at the funeral services for former President Carter on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis asks Georgia’s top court to review decision booting her from Trump case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis (D) has asked Georgia’s top court to review her disqualification from the election subversion case against President-elect Trump and several allies.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, the 'America First' candidate, has a new preoccupation: Imperialism
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump ran on a return to his "America First” foreign policy platform. The U.S., he said, could no longer afford to be the world's policeman. On his watch, he pledged, there would be no new wars.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fani Willis seeks to overturn her disqualification from Trump Georgia election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis has asked Georgia's highest court to review a lower appeals court's ruling that removed her from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: Sept. 11 survivors and victims’ relatives have mixed feelings about a planned plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate Democrats face early test with vote on Laken Riley Act
item.description: Democrats will face an early test on how to navigate immigration under President-elect Trump on Thursday when the Laken Riley Act becomes the first bill to hit the Senate floor.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gingrich predicts Johnson will be Speaker for 6 years
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) is predicting that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will remain in the top job for six years.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to watch ahead of DNC's first leadership race forum
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) is set to host four candidate forums this month as the party looks to replace outgoing Chair Jaime Harrison.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigration firebrand takes reins of the Hispanic Caucus
item.description: Latino Democrats, under new management for the 119th Congress, are looking to flex their muscle under a razor-thin Republican House majority that could make or break President-elect Trump’s immigration agenda. Last week, Rep. Adriano Espaillat (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. stock markets close to honor former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq will both observe Thursday's national day of mourning in a Wall Street tradition dating back to 1865.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: Unfinished Democratic priorities dim along with the party’s hold on power in St. Paul
item.description: A range of DFL proposals moving forward at the Capitol last year are likely frozen in place as Democrats have lost full control of the Legislature when they start back up next week.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: Former U.S. President Jimmy Carter was honored with the pageantry of a funeral in the nation’s capital before a second service in his tiny Georgia hometown that launched a Depression-era farm boy to the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nicole Mitchell’s attorneys push back on effort to bar ‘senator’ mentions at burglary trial
item.description: Ahead of a Minnesota lawmaker’s trial on burglary charges, attorneys are going back and forth over whether she can be called “senator” in front of jurors.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Biden creates 2 new national monuments, setting a conservation record
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden has now protected a total of 674 million acres of lands and waters — a record for any administration. This includes two new national monuments in California.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is held in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a defamation ruling granted to two Georgia poll workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly was in a 'Cabinet meeting' during Mar-a-lago visit
item.description: While Trump's approach is high-risk, O'Reilly argues it reflects his commitment to his agenda and disregard for political norms.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota session agendas start to emerge as power fight drags on, acrimony picks up
item.description: A week ahead of the kick off of the 2025 legislative session, House leaders feuded over who is in charge and what might get accomplished amid a power struggle.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Millions of public workers are set to get higher Social Security benefits. Here's why
item.description: Public service workers, including some teachers, firefighters and police officers, may soon see their Social Security payments increase by hundreds of dollars monthly.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, PIX 11

item.title: President-elect Trump's lawyers ask judge to halt sentencing in hush money case
item.description: Editor's note: The video above is from Jan. 3, 2025. NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump asked a judge Monday to halt this week’s sentencing in his hush money case while they appeal a ruling upholding the verdict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Biden bans new offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters
item.description: President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, an effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
